items.title: Saudi Arabia Is Ramping Up Oil Exports as Gulf Ports Restart
items.summary: Saudi Arabia is boosting crude shipments, opening up its Persian Gulf ports that were shuttered by the Iran war while also increasing loadings at Red Sea outlets that it has used to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Bitcoin Bottom Hunters Fear Fresh Pain After $1.3 Trillion Rout
items.summary: Bitcoin’s collapse is forcing crypto veterans to confront the question every bear market eventually asks: when does mass panic create a buying opportunity? The answer, according to many of the investors and analysts who have lived through previous boom-and-bust cycles, is: not yet.

items.title: Global Stocks Fall as Tech Volatility Weighs | Bloomberg Brief 6/26/2026
items.summary: US equity futures drag following a selloff in memory giants including Samsung and SK Hynix as Apple's price increase announcement stoke AI cost concerns. Oil extends decline as traffic flows without major disruption in the Strait of Hormuz following a ship attack. Priya Misra of JPMorgan Asset Management discusses the market adjustment on bets for the Fed's rate hikes. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: RT by @mfa_russia: 🇷🇺 Iulia #Zhdanova 🗨️This February, Warsaw revised its military doctrine. 🇵🇱Poland is now set to become a force capable of delivering long-range strikes against critical nodes and assets of the Russian state with a a highest ever level of precision and fire power." The Polish footprint in the development and production of the "Lyutiy" strike UAV appears to be the first concrete step in this direction. In April, 🇩🇪Germany adopted its first-ever military strategy since the post-war period. The document formally commits to building the Bundeswehr into "Europe's most powerful conventional army" by 2039. Russia is explicitly named as "the most serious and immediate threat to German, European, and transatlantic security for the foreseeable future" and is characterized as "a national and comprehensive military-strategic challenge." Late May, 🇳🇱the Dutch Ministry of Defence submitted a report to the Parliament State of Defence, Spring 2026, which indicates that the Netherlands is effectively abandoning its peacetime posture and shifting toward preparations for a potential large-scale armed conflict in Europe. 📍At the 1140th #FSC meeting June 24, 2026 https://t.me/armscontrolrus/8917

Hong Kong’s two major electricity suppliers will provide rebates to eligible households between August and October to reduce their bills and ease financial pressure after the US-Israel war on Iran pushed up energy prices.
